title: How to Fix WiFi Not Working on Linux
description: Step-by-step troubleshooting guide for wireless connectivity issues on Linux — from missing drivers to firmware fixes.
prerequisites: Ethernet or USB tethering for downloading drivers

steps.title: Check if your WiFi adapter is detected
steps.content: First, let's see if Linux recognizes your WiFi hardware at all. The `lspci` command shows PCI devices (internal cards), and `lsusb` shows USB devices (USB WiFi dongles).
steps.code:
lspci | grep -i network
lsusb | grep -i wireless

steps.title: Check the current network status
steps.content: Use `nmcli` (NetworkManager CLI) to see the state of your network interfaces. Look for your wireless device — if it says 'unavailable' or is missing, the driver isn't loaded.
steps.code:
nmcli device status
nmcli radio wifi

steps.title: Make sure WiFi is not blocked
steps.content: Some laptops have a hardware or software WiFi kill switch. The `rfkill` command shows if your wireless is blocked. If it says 'soft blocked', unblock it.
steps.code:
rfkill list
sudo rfkill unblock wifi

steps.title: Install missing firmware and drivers
steps.content: The most common cause of WiFi issues is missing firmware. Install the common firmware packages, which cover most Broadcom, Intel, and Realtek adapters.
steps.code:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install linux-firmware firmware-misc-nonfree
sudo apt install bcmwl-kernel-source
steps.warning: You'll need an internet connection (Ethernet or USB tethering) to download these packages.

steps.title: Try the additional drivers tool
steps.content: Ubuntu and its derivatives have a built-in tool that detects your hardware and offers proprietary drivers. Open **Software & Updates** → **Additional Drivers** tab, or run it from the terminal.

steps.title: Restart NetworkManager
steps.content: After installing drivers, restart the network service to pick up the changes. If that doesn't work, a full reboot usually does.
steps.code:
sudo systemctl restart NetworkManager
# If still not working:
sudo reboot

steps.title: Connect to your network
steps.content: Once your adapter is detected, connect to your WiFi network from the terminal or the GUI.
steps.code:
nmcli device wifi list
nmcli device wifi connect "YourNetworkName" password "YourPassword"

seo.title: Fix WiFi Not Working on Linux — Step-by-Step Troubleshooting
seo.description: WiFi not working on Linux? Follow this guide to diagnose and fix wireless issues — missing drivers, firmware, rfkill blocks, and more.
